content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base structure that can be upgraded by integrating multiple inconsistent perspective feedback from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-refinement system that outperforms both original single-perspective Self-Refine and isolated multi-perspective reflection methods. This hybrid system retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while resolving its common flaw of getting stuck in local optimization loops caused by consistent single-perspective feedback.
